reason: The item cleanly targets a single mechanistic axis—how vent/exit geometry changes jet momentum transfer and thus solid entrainment—while constraining away common confounds (same capacity, full runaway, sealed enclosure, comparable total mass ejected). Decoys are mostly plausible and differentiate between geometry-driven entrainment vs substituted mechanisms (gas amount, chemistry, heat retention), though one correct option (F) leans more on definitional inference than mechanism, slightly weakening discriminative sharpness.
